title: rename storage location but cannot find images
body:
Using podman with version of 3.0.2-dev.
The old path of graphRoot is "/var/lib/docker", and I rename it as "/var/lib/podman", then modify graphRoot correspondingly in the file of "/etc/containers/storage.conf". But still cannot get images when I type "podman images", but "podman ps -a" could show all the containers and cannot start.
Moreover, don't know why the graphRoot of "podman info" still point at the old path of "/var/lib/docker".

comments.body:
Please provide all requested data in the issue template, most importantly, the output of `podman info --debug`.

Are you running as a rootless user?  Maybe there is a storage.conf for your specific user in `$HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf`?
podman stores this information in the database. You cannot change the path without running `podman system reset` to delete everything.

> Please provide all requested data in the issue template, most importantly, the output of `podman info --debug`.
> 
> Are you running as a rootless user? Maybe there is a storage.conf for your specific user in `$HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf`?

Running with root user

> podman stores this information in the database. You cannot change the path without running `podman system reset` to delete everything.

It's strange that another machine don't need to run "podman system reset" and the modification is valid, "podman info" will show the updated information.
closing the issue since moving the graphroot is not supported
You mean user cannot customize the graphroot?
you can customize it, but you cannot move an existing one to a different location
